title: 5 to 18: Why Your Count Might Be Off by One
author: Ruslan Spivak
summary: Quick question: How many numbers are there from 5 to 18, including both ends? Your first instinct might be to subtract: 18 - 5 = 13 Feels right. But it’s wrong. It’s a small thing, and kind of basic, but this mistake got me more times than I’d like to admit. Eventually I learned how to count ranges properly. :)…

author: Ruslan Spivak
summary: “Mathematics is the art of reducing any problem to linear algebra.” — William Stein If you’ve ever looked at a vector and thought, “Just a column of numbers, right?”, this chapter will change that. The Dark Art of Linear Algebra (aka DALA) by Seth Braver opens with one of the clearest intros I’ve read. Not every part clicks on the…

title: Book Notes: Full Frontal Calculus by Seth Braver — Chapter 1 Review
author: Ruslan Spivak
summary: “Where there is life, there is change; where there is change, there is calculus.” — Seth Braver I recently went back to studying math to rebuild my foundations for AI and machine learning. I didn’t expect to enjoy a calculus book this much. Shocking, I know. But that’s exactly what happened with Full Frontal Calculus. Can calculus feel intuitive? Even…

title: 7 Things That Helped Me Grow as a Software Engineer
author: Ruslan Spivak
summary: Hi everyone, Growth as a software engineer is an ongoing journey. Looking back, a few key principles helped me progress during the early days of my career.
